identity.officialCurrentName: GR-1（傅利叶第一代通用人形机器人 / Fourier's 1st-generation general-purpose humanoid）
identity.variant: GR-1 (generic entry; possible Pro/Lite sub-variants unresolved)
identity.reason: As of 2026-07-17, GR-1 has NO current dedicated product page, spec sheet, brochure link, or developer-docs section on Fourier's live site. Direct checks today of every previously-cited GR-1 URL returned 404: https://www.fftai.com/products-gr1, the GR-1 brochure PDF, and the GR-1 exhibition case page (fftai.com/solutions/9). The current Developer Center homepage (support.fftai.com/en/) explicitly lists 'GR-1 Coming Soon' (i.e., no current GR-1 docs). GR-1 is mentioned ONLY on the current 'About GRx' history page (https://www.fftai.com/grx/about), which frames it as '傅利叶第一代通用人形机器人' (Fourier's 1st-generation general-purpose humanoid) within an explicit three-generation lineage (GR-1 → GR-2 → GR-3 Series, '历经三次技术迭代'). This is a positive, current, official statement that GR-1 is a past generation of an actively iterating product line, not simply an absence of information — hence 'superseded' rather than 'unknown', and 'archive' rather than 'manual-review' (which is reserved for unexplained vanishing with no generational framing at all).

identity.officialCurrentName: GR-2（傅利叶第二代通用人形机器人 / Fourier's 2nd-generation general-purpose humanoid）
identity.reason: As of 2026-07-17, GR-2 has no current dedicated marketing page (fftai.com/products-gr2 and /products-gr2.html both 404 today) and no current Developer Center section ('GR-2 Coming Soon' on support.fftai.com/en/). GR-2 is described only on the current 'About GRx' history page as '傅利叶第二代通用人形机器人' (2nd generation), explicitly positioned between GR-1 and the current GR-3 Series in a stated three-generation lineage. Unlike GR-1, however, GR-2 IS still explicitly listed as a currently-supported model in the actively-maintained Aurora SDK (GitHub, v1.3.0) alongside GR-3 — i.e. there is live current evidence of continued software/developer support for already-deployed GR-2 units, even though there is no current sales/marketing page. This nuance is noted but does not change the overall lifecycle call, since marketAvailability/product-page presence (not SDK legacy support) governs 'current' vs 'superseded' per this project's definitions.
